message:
refactor(service): extract BufferFuture poll helpers with transition guard

The BufferFuture::poll implementation was a large monolithic match block
that duplicated the pending-slot release logic in multiple arms. On panic
or unexpected early return, the pending counter could leak permanently,
eventually starving the buffer.

This refactors into three focused methods:
- poll_waiting_for_ready: handles the WaitingForReady state, acquires
  the inner service lock, and transitions to Active or Error
- poll_active: polls the inner future and transitions to Done
- release_pending_slot: centralized slot release with waker notification

A BufferTransitionGuard (RAII) wraps each state transition. It holds an
Arc<SharedBuffer> and an armed flag. If the guard is dropped while still
armed (panic during transition), it automatically releases the pending
slot, preventing counter leaks.

The main poll() loop now uses std::mem::replace to take ownership of the
state, dispatches to the appropriate helper, and loops if the helper
returns None (indicating a state transition that requires re-polling).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
